title: Kundalini and Electronics: Why Your Phone Keeps Glitching During Awakening

body:
Kundalini and Electronics: Why Your Phone Keeps Glitching During Awakening

You're up, aren't you? It's 3 AM, your heart's doing a weird little jig, and your phone just froze for the third time tonight. You're probably thinking, "What the actual hell is going on?" I know that feeling. I've been there, staring at a dead screen, wondering if I was losing my mind or if the universe was just having a laugh at my expense. It's not a laugh, I promise you. It's a sign. A weird, annoying, sometimes terrifying sign.

You're going through something big. Something that changes everything. And yeah, it messes with your electronics. You're not imagining it. You're not crazy. You're just experiencing what happens when a whole lot of internal energy starts moving, and your external world - especially the electronic bits - can't quite keep up. Or maybe it's reacting to it. Whatever it is, it's real. And you're not alone in it.

I remember one night, I swear my laptop screen started flickering in sync with my heart palpitations. I was trying to research what was happening to me - this whole kundalini thing - and the damn thing just kept rebooting. Over and over. I wanted to throw it across the room, but I was too weak, too scared, too utterly exhausted. You know that feeling, right? That deep, bone-weary exhaustion that comes with feeling like you're being torn apart and put back together again, usually without your permission.

So yeah, let's talk about why your phone, your laptop, your car, even your freaking lightbulbs, seem to be staging a rebellion against your existence right now.

The Energy Field You're Becoming

Here's the thing: you're not just flesh and bones. You're energy. A whole lot of it. And when kundalini starts to wake up, that energy amplifies. It's like you're suddenly running on a higher voltage than you're used to. Think of it like this: your body and energy system are designed for a certain level of electrical current. When kundalini fires up, it's like someone just plugged you into a power plant instead of a wall socket. Your internal circuits are trying to adapt, sometimes violently, and that overflow, that new frequency, it doesn't just stay inside you. It radiates out.

You become, for lack of a better term, an energetic guide. And electronics? They're sensitive to electromagnetic fields, aren't they? That's how they work. They send and receive signals, process information based on electrical currents. So when you're suddenly emitting a stronger, more erratic, or simply different kind of field, they react. They glitch. They freeze. They die. They restart. It's not personal, though it sure feels like it when you're trying to call for help or just scroll through comforting cat videos.

I've heard countless stories, and I've experienced it myself. Phones dropping calls for no reason, batteries draining at an impossible speed, Wi-Fi cutting out only when you're near it, streetlights flickering as you walk under them - yeah, that's a classic. Even car engines sputtering when you get in. It's all part of the same weird puzzle. Your energy field is changing, and everything around you that runs on electricity is getting a taste of it. It's a mess, I know. It's also a clear indication that something very real is happening to you, beyond just your mind playing tricks.

What's Really Going On with Your Field?

Look, science doesn't have a neat little box for kundalini. Not yet, anyway. But what we know about energy, about the human body's electrical impulses, about the way everything is interconnected - it points to this being a real phenomenon. You're generating a stronger bio-electromagnetic field. Your nervous system is undergoing a massive re-wiring. It's like your internal supercomputer is getting a major software and hardware upgrade all at once, and it's throwing off sparks.

Your brainwaves shift. Your heart's electrical activity changes. Your entire cellular structure is buzzing with new life, new information, new frequencies. And these aren't subtle changes. These are massive, fundamental shifts. So, when your phone, which relies on a precise set of frequencies and electrical currents, suddenly finds itself in the presence of an incredibly powerful, somewhat chaotic, and rapidly fluctuating field - yours - it's no wonder it freaks out.

Think about it. You're not just a passive receiver anymore. You're becoming an active transmitter, and sometimes, a bit of a disruptor. It's not always comfortable, and it's certainly not convenient. But it's part of the process. It's a physical manifestation of the internal changes happening. It's a reminder that this isn't just "in your head," even though your head might be screaming that it is.

The Frustration and the Fear

I know the frustration. When you're already feeling vulnerable, isolated, and maybe even a little bit insane, having your lifeline - your phone - constantly fail you, it's maddening. You just want to connect, to reach out, to find answers, or maybe just distract yourself for a few minutes. And then BLIP, it dies. Or freezes. Or just won't cooperate. It feels like the universe is actively working against you, doesn't it?

And the fear. Oh, the fear. Is this a sign you're truly losing it? Is it some kind of external attack? Are you damaging your devices? You're probably asking all these questions. I did. I thought maybe I was radiating some kind of destructive force. I worried I'd short-circuit myself, or worse, someone else. It's easy to go down that rabbit hole when your reality is already stretched thin.

But here's the truth: you're not damaging anything. Not really. Your devices are just reacting to an unusual energetic presence. They're not designed for this. Most people don't suddenly become walking electromagnetic anomalies. You're unique in this experience, right now. And it's okay to be frustrated. It's okay to be scared. Acknowledge those feelings. Don't fight them. They're valid.

What helps, honestly, is understanding that it's part of the process. It's a symptom, not a cause. The cause is the massive internal awakening happening within you. The electronic glitches are just a side effect, like a fever during an infection. Annoying, yes. But ultimately, an indication that your system is actively working to transform itself.

If you're looking for more insight into the whole wild ride, I found The Kundalini Experience by Lee Sannella (paid link) to be a real eye-opener. It helped me feel less alone, just knowing others had similar physical and energetic experiences. And for the broader picture of what you're going through, When Spirit Leaps: Navigating the Process of Spiritual Awakening by Bonnie Greenwell (paid link) was a lifeline.

Coping Strategies (Because You Need Them)

So, what can you do? You can't exactly turn off your kundalini, can you? And you probably still need your phone. It's a balancing act. Here are a few things I've learned, and what others have found helpful:

  • Distance: When you're feeling particularly intense, try to create some physical distance between yourself and your electronics. Put your phone in another room. Turn off your laptop when you're not actively using it. Give yourself and your devices a break from your amplified field.
  • Grounding: This is huge. Absolutely massive. Grounding helps stabilize your own energy, which can in turn reduce the erratic output. Spend time in nature - barefoot on the grass, hugging a tree, sitting by water. Eat earthy foods. Get enough sleep. Meditate with a focus on sending energy down into the earth. There are tons of grounding exercises out there; find what works for you.
  • Shielding (Sort Of): I'm not talking about tin foil hats here, though if it makes you feel better, go for it. What I mean is creating a sense of energetic protection. Visualize a bubble of calming, protective energy around you. Some people find certain crystals helpful for stabilizing energy, like Black Tourmaline or Shungite. It's more about intention and feeling safe than any magic bullet.
  • Backup Devices: If your phone is constantly dying, maybe keep an old flip phone charged for emergencies. Or a landline, if you still have one. You need reliable ways to communicate, especially when you're feeling fragile.
  • Patience (with yourself and your tech): This isn't forever. Your system is adjusting. It takes time. Your electronics are just reflecting that adjustment. Try not to get too angry at them. They're just doing their best in a weird situation.
  • Charge devices away from your sleeping area: Your energy is often most active when you're resting. Charging your phone right next to your head might not be the best idea if it's already glitching. Give it some space.

Remember, this isn't about superstition. It's about energy. You're a complex energetic being, and right now, your energy is undergoing a serious upgrade. It makes sense that things relying on stable, predictable energy might act up. Don't let the tech troubles add to your anxiety. See them as a confirmation, a strange little validation, that what you're experiencing is deeply real.

The Bigger Picture: It's All Connected

This whole experience - the intense physical sensations, the emotional rollercoasters, the spiritual insights, and yes, the electronic glitches - it's all part of the same process. It's your system clearing out old patterns, old traumas, old blockages, and making space for a new, higher vibrational way of being. It's messy. It's uncomfortable. It's often terrifying. But it's also incredibly powerful.

You're not just a human having a spiritual experience; you're a spiritual being having a very intense human experience. And part of that humanity involves interacting with technology. So when your inner world shifts so dramatically, it's going to ripple out into your outer world, including your gadgets. Does that land? Do you see how it fits together?

I know it's hard to hold onto that bigger picture when you're in the thick of it, when you're feeling like you're losing your mind. But try. Try to see these glitches not as failures, but as markers. As weird, inconvenient little signposts on your path. They're telling you, "Yep, it's still happening. You're still transforming."

For more specific guidance on kundalini and its many manifestations, The Kundalini Guide by Bonnie Greenwell (paid link) is another excellent book. It really breaks down the different stages and symptoms. And if you're feeling completely overwhelmed by the intensity, and questioning your sanity (which is totally normal, by the way), The Stormy Search for the Self by Stanislav & Christina Grof (paid link) can provide some much-needed context on spiritual emergencies and the broader spectrum of human experience.

You're strong. Stronger than you think. You're going through something incredible, even if it feels like hell right now. The electronic glitches are just a small, strange part of that journey. Hang in there. You're not alone. You're going to get through this. One glitchy phone, one sleepless night, one weird energy surge at a time.

This is not a substitute for professional mental health care. If you're in crisis, please reach out to a qualified therapist or call 988.
author: Kalesh
image_alt: Kundalini and Electronics: Why Your Phone Keeps Glitching During Awakening
